# gogit-interop

A .NET wrapper around go-git, using CGo P/Invoke to expose a Go shared library (`libgogit.dylib`/`.so`/`.dll`) to C#.

## Project structure

```
generate/          Go code generator (source of truth for all generated files)
shared/            Generated Go CGo exports + libgogit.h (DO NOT EDIT)
dotnet/GoGitDotNet/  Generated C# wrapper classes (DO NOT EDIT)
dotnet/GoGitDotNet.Tests/  Hand-written tests
```

## CRITICAL: Never edit generated files directly

All files that begin with `// Code generated by gogit-interop/generate. DO NOT EDIT.` are **regenerated** by `make generate` and will be overwritten. **Any direct edits to these files will be lost** the next time the generator runs (or when the IDE/linter refreshes them).

This includes:
- `dotnet/GoGitDotNet/*.cs` (Blob.cs, Commit.cs, Repository.cs, Worktree.cs, etc.)
- `dotnet/GoGitDotNet/Options/*.cs`
- `dotnet/GoGitDotNet/Iterators/*.cs`
- `dotnet/GoGitDotNet/Models/*.cs`
- `dotnet/GoGitDotNet/NativeMethods.cs`
- `shared/worktree_gen.go` and other `*_gen.go` files
- `shared/libgogit.h`

## Making changes — always edit the generator

To add or change behaviour, modify the generator in `generate/` then run `make generate`:

| What you want to change | Where to edit |
|---|---|
| C# wrapper class methods (Blob, Commit, etc.) | `generate/gen_csharp_wrappers.go` |
| C# options classes (CloneOptions, FetchOptions, etc.) | `generate/gen_csharp_options.go` |
| Hand-crafted C# classes (Auth, Signer, iterators) | `generate/gen_csharp_special.go` |
| Go CGo export functions | `generate/gen_go.go` |
| Go options exports | `generate/gen_go_options.go` |
| Method overrides / skip list | `generate/overrides.go` |
| Type name / method name mappings | `generate/mappings.go` |
| Data model (which go-git types to wrap) | `generate/model.go` |

When updating the generator, review the changes introduced to consider if the changes are purely organisational, and if it has no impacts the the final compiled code. We want to avoid unecessary commits and diffs when making non-impactful changes. For example avoid reorganizing the imports unless there is an important side effect of doing so.

After editing the generator, run:
```
make generate       # regenerates shared/ and dotnet/GoGitDotNet/
make build          # recompiles libgogit (required if Go exports changed)
cd dotnet && dotnet test GoGitDotNet.Tests/GoGitDotNet.Tests.csproj
```

## Test files are hand-written

Files under `dotnet/GoGitDotNet.Tests/` are **not generated** and should be edited directly.

Since these files are used to ensure the consistency of the API, ensure that you always confirm changes made to these tests.

## Build pipeline

```
make generate       # Go → generates shared/*.go + dotnet/GoGitDotNet/*.cs
make                # compiles libgogit.dylib for the current platform
cd dotnet && dotnet test   # runs C# tests against the compiled library
```
